headline: Man Utd stance over Casemiro U-turn revealed after Michael Carrick resurgence
snippet: Casemiro is expected to stick to his guns and leave Manchester United at the end of the season (Picture: Getty) Manchester United are reportedly yet to hold talks with Casemiro to try to convince the
full_text: Casemiro is expected to stick to his guns and leave Manchester United at the end of the season (Picture: Getty) Manchester United are reportedly yet to hold talks with Casemiro to try to convince the veteran midfielder to change his mind and stay beyond the summer. Casemiro made an official announcement back in late January that this would be his fourth and final season at Old Trafford. The 34-year-old midfielder joined United from Real Madrid in a deal worth around £70million in 2022 and has gone on to make 154 appearances in all competitions for the Red Devils. Given the fanfare surrounding his arrival in the Premier League, it’s fair to say Casemiro has – at times – fallen short of expectations, not helped by United’s struggles as a whole under Erik ten Hag and subsequently Ruben Amorim. But since Michael Carrick took over the reins from Amorim, Casemiro is playing some of his best football yet in a United shirt and calls for the Brazilian to make a U-turn on his decision are growin...
